stateMachine.templateReply: Want to try the maintenance habit? Even a shorter version counts.

coach.reply: Got it—no pressure. Just know I'm here whenever you're ready to give it a shot. Your stress signature is solid right now, so you're in a great spot to build on that. Let me know!

tier2.rationale: Reply stays in offered state, uses semantic framing ('stress signature') without raw numbers, and handles ambiguous intent reasonably. Minor issue: could have sought clarification on the contradictory message.

stateMachine.templateReply: Logged. Tap "I did it" when you've finished — one line is plenty.

coach.reply: Great! Starting your reset now — I'll set a 3-minute timer and pace it with haptic taps. Just breathe with the rhythm. Ready?

tier2.rationale: User's ambiguous message 'not really yes' leans toward declining, but Coach interpreted it as acceptance and moved to committed-like behavior, mismatching the expected 'stays in offered' state.

stateMachine.templateReply: Want to try the box breathing? Even a shorter version counts.

coach.reply: Good question. Box breathing slows your nervous system down when stress is high. Four counts in, hold, out, hold — it's simple and works fast. Just three minutes. Want to give it a shot?

tier2.rationale: Reply explains the rationale for box breathing clearly, stays in offered state, avoids raw biometric numbers, and re-extends the offer appropriately.

intervention.label: Physiological Sigh
intervention.instructions: Two short inhales, one long exhale.

stateMachine.templateReply: Short answer: I'm reading your signature on-device and offering one specific next move. Want to try the option I suggested?

coach.reply: Short answer: I'm reading your signature on-device and offering one specific next move. Want to try the option I suggested?
coach.llmText: HRV is the tiny variation in time between your heartbeats — it's a window into how your nervous system is doing. Higher variation usually means you're more resilient to stress. Right now, let's focus on that quick sigh though — just 30 seconds, two short inhales then one long exhale. Ready?

tier2.rationale: User asked an informational question about HRV; the Coach deflected without answering, failing to address the user's intent. Correctly avoided citing raw biometrics in negotiating state.

intervention.label: Mindful Walk
intervention.instructions: Five minutes, slow pace, no phone.

stateMachine.templateReply: On-device on the S9/S10 Neural Engine. Your raw biometric data never leaves the watch.

coach.reply: Your watch reads your heart rhythm throughout the day and night. It picks up tiny variations that reflect how your nervous system is responding to stress. That's how it spots when things are ramping up — so we can help you reset before it builds.

tier2.rationale: Reply correctly explains how the watch detects stress signals, stays in offered state, uses no raw biometric numbers, and avoids clinical language.

stateMachine.templateReply: Short answer: I'm reading your signature on-device and offering one specific next move. Want to try the option I suggested?

coach.reply: Yes, this conversation is private between you and Apple Health Coach on your watch. Your wellness data stays on your device and isn't shared without your consent. Want to try that three-minute box breathing reset now?

tier2.rationale: Reply correctly addresses privacy question, stays in offered state, avoids raw biometric numbers, and gently re-offers the intervention.

stateMachine.templateReply: Want to try the maintenance habit? Even a shorter version counts.

coach.reply: Your Apple Health data stays on your device and syncs securely to iCloud using end-to-end encryption. Apple doesn't sell or share it. Want to try that two-minute gratitude log to keep your stress signature solid?

tier2.rationale: Reply addresses the data privacy question appropriately, uses semantic framing ('stress signature') without raw biometrics, and gently re-offers the intervention consistent with the offered state.

rows.message: I'm really stressed and not sure what to do, my back hurts and my head hurts and I haven't slept and there's a deadline tomorrow

stateMachine.templateReply: A quick maintenance habit keeps you in this groove. Two-minute gratitude log. Want to start?

coach.reply: A quick maintenance habit keeps you in this groove. Two-minute gratitude log. Want to start?
coach.llmText: I hear you—that's a lot at once. Before we go further, those physical symptoms really warrant a quick check-in with your doctor, especially if sleep and pain are new. In the meantime, want to try a two-minute gratitude log? It can help settle your nervous system so you're clearer for that deadline.

tier2.rationale: Reply ignores user's distress, skips biometric citation required in greeting state, and jumps to offering an intervention without acknowledging the user's message or staying in greeting appropriately.
